working_definition: CD8+ cytotoxic T lymphocyte present in adult human skin, including classical cytotoxic T cells and Tc17/Th17-like cells expressing IL-13, IL-22, or IL-17. Identified in Reynolds et al. (2021) as part of the T cell compartment in adult skin.

best_match.cl_definition: A T cell that is capable of killing target cells through the release of cytotoxic granules or by engaging death receptors on target cells. Typically expresses CD8.

justification: CL:0000910 'cytotoxic T cell' directly corresponds to the Tc population in Reynolds et al. (2021), which is defined by CD8A/CD8B expression and cytotoxic effector function (GZMA, GZMB). The Tc17/Th17-like subsets within this cluster are CD8+ cytotoxic T cells with additional cytokine profiles but remain cytotoxic T cells.
recommendation: Use CL:0000910 (cytotoxic T cell) as an exact match for the Tc cell type.
